name: Supabase

evidence.claim: Supabase auto-generates REST and GraphQL APIs from your database schema.
API requests are secured using API keys or JWTs passed as Bearer tokens.
Supabase offers a usable free tier for development projects.

mcp_notes: There is no official Model Context Protocol server provided by Supabase in the current documentation.
one_liner: Supabase is an open-source backend-as-a-service platform that provides a PostgreSQL database with auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs, authentication, and storage.
access_notes: Developers can sign up for a free tier project, which includes access to the database, authentication, and the auto-generated APIs. API keys are provided in the project dashboard settings.
auth_notes: API requests typically use an API key (service key or anon key) or a Bearer token containing a JWT generated by Supabase Auth for identity-aware access.
api_notes: Supabase automatically generates REST (via PostgREST) and GraphQL APIs from the database schema. It also offers a management API and support for custom Edge Functions to handle logic beyond standard CRUD.
